title: Heat waves, droughts, and preferences for environmental policy
authors: Ann L. Owen, Emily Conover, Julio Videras, Stephen Wu

excluded_policy_mentions.exact_term_used_in_paper: Government should pass laws to make ordinary people protect the environment, even if it interferes with people's right to make their own decisions.
excluded_policy_mentions.reason_it_is_not_a_carbon_or_fuel_tax: This is general environmental regulation, not a carbon or fuel tax.

inclusion_reason: Examines how weather affects support for environmental regulation and global warming importance, but it does not analyze any carbon or fuel tax instrument.
exclusion_reason: No direct analysis of carbon/fuel tax acceptability or support; the policy item is a general law to protect the environment.
